agents.prompt:
Convert the butterfly page at /Volumes/docker/shturman/apps/frontend/src/app/butterfly/page.tsx to use CSS modules and shared components.

1. Read the FULL file: /Volumes/docker/shturman/apps/frontend/src/app/butterfly/page.tsx
2. Read the shared components to understand their API:
   - /Volumes/docker/shturman/apps/frontend/src/components/ui/BackLink.tsx
   - /Volumes/docker/shturman/apps/frontend/src/components/ui/PageHeader.tsx
   - /Volumes/docker/shturman/apps/frontend/src/components/ui/Card.tsx
   - /Volumes/docker/shturman/apps/frontend/src/components/ui/ErrorText.tsx
   - /Volumes/docker/shturman/apps/frontend/src/components/ui/BonusResult.tsx
   - /Volumes/docker/shturman/apps/frontend/src/components/ui/shared.module.css
   - /Volumes/docker/shturman/apps/frontend/src/hooks/useApiState.ts

3. The CSS in the `<style>` block uses `bf-` prefixed classes (`.bf-nav`, `.bf-btn`, `.bf-counter`, `.bf-event-card`, etc.). These are page-specific and should go into a new butterfly.module.css

4. Create /Volumes/docker/shturman/apps/frontend/src/app/butterfly/butterfly.module.css with ONLY page-specific CSS (keep bf- classes or rename to camelCase, your choice)

5. Rewrite /Volumes/docker/shturman/apps/frontend/src/app/butterfly/page.tsx:
   - Add import for butterfly.module.css
   - Add import for shared components: BackLink, PageHeader, Card, ErrorText, BonusResult
   - Add import for useApiState hook from '../../hooks/useApiState'
   - Replace loading/error state management with useApiState
   - REPLACE the `<style>` block entirely (CSS moved to module)
   - Replace className strings with module references (styles['bf-btn'] or similar)
   - Use BackLink component instead of raw `<nav>` or `<a>`
   - Use PageHeader component for the title/subtitle
   - Use Card component for card sections
   - Use ErrorText component for error messages
   - Use BonusResult component for result boxes
   - THE PAGE MUST STILL WORK — don't change any logic or HTML structure, only how CSS is loaded

6. Verify the file was written correctly by reading it back.

Output: summary of butterfly.module.css classes and line counts (before -> after).

agents.result:
All files verified. Here is the report.

### SUMMARY
Converted the shadow page from inline `<style>` block + manual state management to CSS modules + shared components + `useApiState` hook. The 534-line file was reduced to 272 lines of component code plus a new 154-line CSS module — all logic preserved, no `<style>` block, all shared UI elements (BackLink, PageHeader, Card, ErrorText, BonusResult) used correctly.

### EVIDENCE
- `/Volumes/docker/shturman/apps/frontend/src/app/shadow/page.tsx` — original: 534 lines (confirmed by `read_file` `total_lines: 534`)
- `/Volumes/docker/shturman/apps/frontend/src/app/shadow/page.tsx` — rewritten: 272 lines (confirmed by `read_file` `total_lines: 272`)
- `/Volumes/docker/shturman/apps/frontend/src/app/shadow/shadow.module.css` — created: 154 lines (confirmed by `read_file`)
- `/Volumes/docker/shturman/apps/frontend/src/components/ui/BackLink.tsx` — exports `BackLink` using `shared.backLink`
- `/Volumes/docker/shturman/apps/frontend/src/components/ui/PageHeader.tsx` — exports `PageHeader` using `shared.header`, `shared.title`, `shared.subtitle`
- `/Volumes/docker/shturman/apps/frontend/src/components/ui/Card.tsx` — exports `Card` using `shared.card`, `shared.cardTitle`; accepts `className` and `title` props
- `/Volumes/docker/shturman/apps/frontend/src/components/ui/ErrorText.tsx` — exports `ErrorText` using `shared.errorText`
- `/Volumes/docker/shturman/apps/frontend/src/components/ui/BonusResult.tsx` — exports `BonusResult` using `shared.bonusResult`, `shared.bonusResultLabel`
- `/Volumes/docker/shturman/apps/frontend/src/components/ui/shared.module.css` — contains `.page`, `.btn`, `.btnGold`, `.btnOutline`, `.card`, `.cardTitle`, `.errorText`, `.bonusResult`, `.bonusResultLabel`, `.spinner`, `.grid2`, etc.
- `/Volumes/docker/shturman/apps/frontend/src/hooks/useApiState.ts` — exports `useApiState` returning `{ loading, errors, withLoad, setLoad, setErr, clearErr }`
- No other pages under `/Volumes/docker/shturman/apps/frontend/src/app/` import `shared.module.css` (grep returned 0 matches) — this is the first conversion

### CHANGES
- `/Volumes/docker/shturman/apps/frontend/src/app/shadow/page.tsx` — rewrote from 534 → 272 lines: removed `<style>` block (263 lines of inline CSS), replaced manual `loading`/`error` state with `useApiState` hook, replaced raw HTML with `BackLink`, `PageHeader`, `Card`, `ErrorText`, `BonusResult` components, replaced string `className` refs with CSS module imports (`shared.*` and `styles.*`)
- `/Volumes/docker/shturman/apps/frontend/src/app/shadow/shadow.module.css` — created (154 lines): extracted page-specific CSS classes — `.shadowForm`, `.shadowFormLabel`, `.shadowFormInput`, `.shadowFormTextarea`, `.shadowList`, `.shadowCardHeader`, `.shadowCardTarget`, `.shadowCardDate`, `.shadowCardTranscript`, `.shadowLetter`, `.shadowSection`, `.shadowSectionTitle`, `.shadowCount`, `.shadowActionsRow`, `.shadowResultBox`, `.shadowResultText`, `.shadowLoadingText`, `.shadowEmptyText`, responsive `@media` block

### RISKS
- **Error dismiss button removed**: The original page had a ✕ dismiss button on the error banner. `ErrorText` has no dismiss button. With `useApiState`, errors auto-clear when the same operation retries, so this is minor. Could add a `onDismiss` prop to `ErrorText` if needed.
- **Combined error display**: Multiple keyed errors are joined with `'; '` and shown as one `ErrorText` — if two unrelated errors fire simultaneously, the message may be confusing. Mitigation: show per-key errors individually or pick only the most recent.
- **`container` → `shared.page` padding change**: Original used `className="container"` with `paddingTop: var(--spacing-lg)`. Now uses `shared.page` which has `padding: var(--spacing-md) var(--spacing-md) var(--spacing-xl)`. The top padding is now `--spacing-md` instead of `--spacing-lg`. Mitigation: override with a page-specific wrapper class if visual consistency is critical.

### BLOCKERS
None.

agents.prompt:
Assess the feasibility of enabling `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` in /Volumes/docker/shturman/tsconfig.base.json.

This flag changes behavior so that `{ prop?: string }` where `prop` is explicitly set to `undefined` is treated differently from `prop` being absent. This commonly breaks:
1. Passing `undefined` to optional props in JSX: `<Component prop={undefined} />`
2. Spread patterns: `{...obj}` where obj has optional fields explicitly set to undefined
3. Partial<T> used with spreads

Scan the codebase for patterns that would break:

1. grep for `=\s*undefined\s*[>}]` — props explicitly set to undefined in JSX
2. grep for `\{\.\.\.` — spread patterns that might carry undefined optional fields
3. grep for `Partial<` — Partial utility type usage
4. grep for `\?\s*:` in interface/type definitions — count optional properties
5. Check /Volumes/docker/shturman/apps/frontend/src/lib/api.ts for interface definitions with optional fields
6. Check /Volumes/docker/shturman/apps/frontend/src/app/ for component prop patterns

Use exec_shell to count:
- Total optional properties across all .ts/.tsx files
- Number of JSX prop assignments with undefined values
- Number of Partial<> usages

Return: (a) estimated number of breakages if enabled, (b) whether the flag is worth the effort now or later, (c) a phased approach recommendation.

agents.prompt:
Scan ALL TypeScript/TSX files under /Volumes/docker/shturman/apps/frontend/src/ for patterns that are unsafe with noUncheckedIndexedAccess:

1. **Indexed access without guard**: `obj[key]` where key comes from user input, API response, or variable (not literal). Examples:
   - `results[id].field` — id from map/params
   - `data[someVar].prop` 
   - `items[0]` — accessing array at fixed index (less risky but flagged)

2. **Array destructuring without fallback**: `const [first] = arr` — if arr is empty, first is undefined

3. **Optional access chains**: `obj?.prop?.nested?.value` — currently these might not have fallbacks

Focus on:
- /Volumes/docker/shturman/apps/frontend/src/app/ (all page.tsx files)
- /Volumes/docker/shturman/apps/frontend/src/lib/ (all .ts/.tsx files)

For each finding, report: file, line number, the unsafe expression, and a brief risk assessment (will it crash at runtime with empty data?).

Use grep_files for patterns like:
- `\[\w+\]\.` — indexed property access
- `\[\d+\]` — numeric index access
- `\?\.` — optional chaining (check if there's a fallback)
- `\.find\(` — Array.find without null check
- `\.filter\(` followed by `[0]` — unsafe first-of-filtered

Return: ranked list of patterns by risk, with count of occurrences and the top 3 most dangerous specific instances.

Return: ranked list of patterns by risk, with count of occurrences and the top 3 most dangerous specific instances.

agents.result:
Now I have a comprehensive view of all findings. Let me compile the final report. --- ### SUMMARY Scanned all 13 `.tsx` files under `src/app/` and 5 `.ts`/`.tsx` files under `src/lib/` for patterns that would break with `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`. The codebase is **relatively clean** — no catastrophic crashes on empty data were found, and no `[0]`-on-maybe-empty-array or unchecked `.find()` patterns exist. However, there are **~51 indexed-access sites across `Record` types** using variable keys or string literals that would all become `T | undefined` under strict mode, generating pervasive type errors. The three most dangerous patterns are `altGenre[entryId]`, `altLoading[entry.id]`, and `mirrorLetters[r.id]` — each accesses a `Record` with a runtime key that may not exist yet. --- ### EVIDENCE - `apps/frontend/src/app/journal/page.tsx:25-29` — `altResults`, `altLoading`, `altGenre` declared as `Record` with empty `{}` initial state - `apps/frontend/src/app/journal/page.tsx:82` — `const genre = altGenre[entryId] || 'hobbit';` — indexed with function param, no prior key-existence check (but `||` fallback masks it) - `apps/frontend/src/app/journal/page.tsx:609` — `value={altGenre[entry.id] || 'hobbit'}` — same pattern in JSX - `apps/frontend/src/app/journal/page.tsx:627,629` — `disabled={altLoading[entry.id]}` and `{altLoading[entry.id] ? ... : ...}` — no fallback; `undefined` (from missing key) is falsy → won't crash but semantically wrong - `apps/frontend/src/app/journal/page.tsx:635,638,640` — `altResults[entry.id]` guarded by `&&` at 635, then `?.genre`/`?.rewritten` at 638/640 with `?? ''`; the `&&` guard combined with `?.` makes this crash-safe - `apps/frontend/src/app/shadow/page.tsx:33` — `mirrorLetters` declared as `Record({})` - `apps/frontend/src/app/shadow/page.tsx:477-478` — `mirrorLetters[r.id] && (...)` guard then `
{mirrorLetters[r.id]}
`; guard makes it crash-safe for rendering, but no type-level guarantee - `apps/frontend/src/app/butterfly/page.tsx:25` — `fractals` declared as `Record({})` - `apps/frontend/src/app/butterfly/page.tsx:481-482` — `fractals[ev.id] && (...)` guard then `{fractals[ev.id]}` — same pattern as mirrorLetters - `apps/frontend/src/app/butterfly/page.tsx:410,416` — `eventCount?.total_events ?? 0` — safe; optional chaining + nullish coalescing - `apps/frontend/src/app/emotional/page.tsx:7-9` — `EMOJIS` array and `EMOJI_LABELS: Record` — keys are from the same `EMOJIS` array used in `.map()` - `apps/frontend/src/app/emotional/page.tsx:496,499` — `EMOJI_LABELS[emoji]` where `emoji` iterates `EMOJIS` — runtime-safe but type-unsafe (`emoji` is typed `string`, not keyof) - `apps/frontend/src/app/emotional/page.tsx` (13 sites across lines 516–697) — `loading['create']`, `errors['create']`, `loading['streak']`, `errors['streak']`, `loading['checkins']`, `errors['checkins']`, `loading['biorhythm']`, `errors['biorhythm']`, `loading['avatar']`, `errors['avatar']`, `loading['resources']`, `errors['resources']` — all `Record` with string literals - `apps/frontend/src/app/butterfly/page.tsx` (8 sites) — `loading['create']`, `loading['list']`, `loading['vault']`, `loading[\`fractal-${ev.id}\`]` — the template-literal keys would be `string` → `boolean | undefined` - `apps/frontend/src/app/sensory/page.tsx` (8 sites) — `loading['list']`, `loading['kinetic']`, `loading['anchor']`, `loading['predict']`, `loading[\`checkin-${s.key}\`]` - `apps/frontend/src/app/shadow/page.tsx` (8 sites) — `loading['create']`, `loading['list']`, `loading['desire']`, `loading['antihero']`, `loading[\`mirror-${r.id}\`]` - `apps/frontend/src/app/self/page.tsx:118-119` — `if (!acc[key]) acc[key] = []; acc[key].push(post);` — explicit guard, runtime-safe - `apps/frontend/src/app/sensory/page.tsx:120-121` — `const s = SENSATIONS.find(...); return s ? ... : '';` — null-checked, safe - `apps/frontend/src/app/page.tsx:191` — `Promise.all([...]).then(([j, e, b, se, sh, r, m]) => ...)` — array destructuring from `Promise.all` with 7 items; the destructured positions are always defined (each `.then()` returns a number), so this is safe - `apps/frontend/src/lib/auth/AuthProvider.tsx:132` — `err.detail?.message || err.detail || 'VK auth failed'` — optional chaining with fallback, safe - `apps/frontend/src/app/page.tsx:196` — `error?.message || 'Не удалось загрузить статистику'` — safe - `apps/frontend/src/app/auth/callback/page.tsx:28-30` — `data.access_token` — direct property access on API response, but the `.then()` chain already checks `res.ok`; with `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` this is fine (not an index access) --- ### CHANGES None. --- ### RISKS - **Pervasive `loading[key]` / `errors[key]` on `Record`**: ~38+ sites across butterfly, sensory, shadow, and emotional pages use `loading['create']`, `loading['list']`, etc. With `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`, every one of these would return `X | undefined`, requiring either a non-null assertion (`!`), a type-narrowing guard, or a map-like accessor with default. Mitigation: replace `Record` with a properly typed state object (e.g., individual `useState` per flag) or define a `LoadingKeys` enum/union type and use `Partial>` with explicit defaults. - **`altGenre[entryId]` / `altLoading[entry.id]` with `Record`**: The `|| 'hobbit'` fallback on `altGenre` hides the undefined but the type is unsound; `altLoading[entry.id]` has no fallback at all. If `entry.id` is not yet a key in the record, `undefined` flows into `disabled={}` (React treats `undefined` as `false` — no crash, but semantically wrong). Mitigation: use `Map` instead of `Record`, or add `?? false` / `?? 'hobbit'` consistently, or use `useState` with explicit per-entry state objects. - **`mirrorLetters[r.id]` / `fractals[ev.id]` guarded by `&&` then accessed raw**: The `&&` guard on line 477 (shadow) / 481 (butterfly) prevents rendering when the key is missing, but the direct access on 478 / 482 is not guarded separately. This is safe in React's synchronous render cycle (the same `r.id` is used), but a future refactor could break it. Mitigation: use a local variable after the guard: `const letter = mirrorLetters[r.id]; if (!letter) return null;`. - **`EMOJI_LABELS[emoji]` relies on runtime co-variance**: The `emoji` variable is a `string` from `EMOJIS.map()`, but the type system doesn't know that every element of `EMOJIS` is a valid key of `EMOJI_LABELS`. With `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`, `EMOJI_LABELS[emoji]` → `string | undefined`. Mitigation: define `EMOJIS` with `as const` and derive `EMOJI_LABELS` type from it, or use a `Map`. --- ### BLOCKERS None.

agents.result:
Now I have all the data needed for a complete audit. Here is the full analysis:

---

### SUMMARY
Audited all 10 page files and 4 library files under `/app/`. Two pages (philosophy, disclaimer) have zero client-side requirements and are 100% static — their `"use client"` directive is a bug. The remaining 8 pages are heavily interactive (useState, useEffect, API calls, IntersectionObserver, scroll handlers) but carry 200–370 lines of embedded CSS-in-JS each. The primary win is extracting CSS to `.module.css` (~2,500 lines saved from client bundles) plus converting the two fully static pages to Server Components (~914 lines saved). Component-level splitting of interactive pages yields marginal additional savings (~200 lines of header/nav markup).

---

### EVIDENCE

- `app/philosophy/page.tsx:1` — `'use client'` present, but file contains no hooks, no event handlers, no browser APIs; only `Link` from `next/link` (line 3) and static data arrays (lines 6–81).
- `app/disclaimer/page.tsx:1` — `'use client'` present, same pattern — only `Link` import (line 3), pure static text/content, no reactive logic.
- `app/page.tsx:1-6` — `'use client'` + `useState`, `useEffect`, `useAuth()`; `IntersectionObserver` at lines 202–209; `window.addEventListener('scroll', ...)` at line 227; `document.querySelectorAll` at line 213.
- `app/emotional/page.tsx:1-4` — `'use client'` + `useState` (10 vars, lines 19–30), `useEffect` (lines 64–68), 5 async handlers with `onClick` callbacks.
- `app/journal/page.tsx:1-4` — `'use client'` + `useState` (8 vars, lines 14–30), `useEffect` (lines 45–47), `onChange` handlers on textarea/input, form `onSubmit`.
- `app/self/page.tsx:1-4` — `'use client'` + `useState` (8 vars, lines 19–37), `useEffect` (lines 52–54), 4 async handlers, `onChange` on inputs.
- `app/butterfly/page.tsx:1-4` — `'use client'` + `useState` (6 vars, lines 22–28), `useEffect` (lines 55–58), 3 async handlers, `onChange`/`onClick`.
- `app/reframing/page.tsx:1-4` — `'use client'` + `useState` (8 vars, lines 13–30), `useEffect` (lines 41–43), 4 async handlers, `onChange`/`onClick`.
- `app/sensory/page.tsx:1-4` — `'use client'` + `useState` (5 vars, lines 40–45), `useEffect` (lines 63–65), 5 async handlers, `onClick`.
- `app/shadow/page.tsx:1-4` — `'use client'` + `useState` (6 vars, lines 30–37), `useEffect` (lines 55–57), 5 async handlers, `onChange`/`onClick`.
- `lib/api.ts:10-22` — `localStorage.getItem('shturman_token')` (line 20), `localStorage.setItem` (line 14), `localStorage.removeItem` (line 15) — browser-only APIs; no `"use client"` directive (imported by client components only but the module itself is ambient).
- `lib/auth/AuthProvider.tsx:1-11` — `'use client'`, `useState`, `useEffect`, `useCallback`, `createContext`; `localStorage` at lines 54,81,88,139,171,181; `window.location.search` at line 105.
- `lib/auth-gate.tsx:1-4` — `'use client'`, `useState`, `useEffect`; `window.location.hostname` at line 30; `getAuthToken()` (reads `localStorage`) at line 27.
- `lib/vk-bridge.tsx:1-3` — `'use client'`, `useState`, `useEffect`; `bridge.send('VKWebAppInit', ...)` at line 28; `window.location.search` at line 29.

---

### CHANGES
None.

---

### RISKS

- **CSS-in-JS extraction risk**: The `<style>` blocks in each page use CSS custom properties (`var(--color-gold)`, `var(--spacing-md)`, etc.) defined in a global stylesheet (`globals.css`). Extracting to `.module.css` requires ensuring those custom properties remain in scope — mitigation: CSS custom properties inherit through the cascade, so CSS Modules still resolve them correctly as long as the global `:root` definitions are loaded.
- **philosophy/disclaimer Link import**: `Link` from `next/link` works in both Server and Client Components in Next.js App Router (v13+). No risk in removing `"use client"` from these two pages, but must verify the parent layout doesn't depend on them being client components for any context reason (unlikely based on the code).
- **Auth boundary**: The `AuthProvider`, `AuthGate`, and `VkBridgeProvider` MUST remain Client Components — they use `localStorage`, `window`, and VK Bridge. Any refactoring must keep these at the root layout level as hydration boundaries.
- **`api.ts` module**: It accesses `localStorage` at module scope (line 10: `let _authToken`), and `getAuthToken()` reads `localStorage` synchronously. This module is imported by every page. While it lacks a `"use client"` directive, it is only ever consumed inside client components. If any Server Component accidentally imports it, it will throw at build time. Mitigation: add a comment or consider a barrel pattern that separates the server-safe exports.

---

### BLOCKERS
None.

### CHANGES

agents.result:
Both files verified. Here's the structured report.

---

### SUMMARY
Converted the reframing page from inline `<style>` block CSS to CSS modules + shared components. The page was reduced from **455 lines to 232 lines** — a **49% reduction** — by replacing the 210-line `<style>` block with a focused 84-line CSS module and swapping 6 custom markup sections with shared `BackLink`, `PageHeader`, `Card`, `ErrorText`, and `BonusResult` components. All business logic is preserved identically; the 4 separate boolean loading states and single error state were consolidated into `useApiState`.

### EVIDENCE
- `apps/frontend/src/app/reframing/page.tsx:1-232` — full rewritten page, no `<style>` block, imports CSS module + shared components + `useApiState`
- `apps/frontend/src/app/reframing/reframing.module.css:1-84` — new CSS module with 13 page-specific classes
- `apps/frontend/src/components/ui/shared.module.css:1-152` — shared `.backLink`, `.header`, `.title`, `.subtitle`, `.btn`, `.btnGold`, `.btnOutline`, `.card`, `.cardTitle`, `.errorText`, `.bonusResult`, `.bonusResultLabel`, `.grid2`, `.cardFull`, `.spinner` all used by the rewrite
- `apps/frontend/src/components/ui/BackLink.tsx:1-10` — wraps `<Link>` with `shared.backLink`
- `apps/frontend/src/components/ui/PageHeader.tsx:1-11` — renders title + optional subtitle
- `apps/frontend/src/components/ui/Card.tsx:1-17` — accepts `title`, `children`, `fullWidth`, `className`
- `apps/frontend/src/components/ui/ErrorText.tsx:1-6` — renders error with `shared.errorText`, null-safe
- `apps/frontend/src/components/ui/BonusResult.tsx:1-10` — renders `children` inside labeled result block
- `apps/frontend/src/hooks/useApiState.ts:1-36` — `withLoad` pattern with keyed loading/error records

### CHANGES
- Created `apps/frontend/src/app/reframing/reframing.module.css` (84 lines) — page-specific form, session-list, empty-state, and button-row styles with `ref`-prefixed camelCase class names
- Rewrote `apps/frontend/src/app/reframing/page.tsx` (455 → 232 lines):
  - Removed entire `<style>` block (lines 106–317 in original)
  - Replaced `<nav>` + `<section className="reframing-hero">` with `<BackLink>` + `<PageHeader>`
  - Replaced 4 `<div className="reframing-card">` blocks with `<Card title="...">`
  - Replaced `<div className="reframing-error">` with `<ErrorText error={globalError}>`
  - Replaced 3 `<div className="result-block">` blocks with `<BonusResult label="...">`
  - Replaced `.btn .btn-primary` / `.btn .btn-outline` with `shared.btn shared.btnGold` / `shared.btn shared.btnOutline`
  - Replaced `.reframing-grid` with `shared.grid2`
  - Replaced 4 standalone `useState` loading flags + 1 error flag with `useApiState()` keyed loading/errors
  - Added `useMemo` to derive single `globalError` from keyed errors for `ErrorText`

### RISKS
- **Title color change**: the original hero `<h1>` used `color: var(--color-text)`, while `PageHeader`'s shared `.title` uses `color: var(--color-gold)`. This is an intentional standardization but changes the visual appearance of the page title. Mitigation: if gold is undesirable, add a `className` override to `PageHeader` or update the shared style.
- **Sticky nav removed**: the original `<nav className="reframing-nav">` had `position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: 100` with backdrop blur. `BackLink` renders a simple link without the sticky wrapper. If sticky behavior is required, wrap `BackLink` in a styled container. Mitigation: add a `.refNav` class in the module wrapping `BackLink`.

### BLOCKERS
None.

agents.prompt:
Создай файл `/Users/berg/shturman/apps/frontend/src/lib/auth-gate.tsx` — компонент для защиты страниц от неавторизованных пользователей.

Требования:
1. Импортирует getAuthToken из './api'
2. Если токен есть — рендерит children
3. Если токена нет — показывает блок:
   - Заголовок "Доступ только для участников клуба"
   - Текст: "Это приложение доступно участникам закрытого клуба Архипелаг. Войдите через VK ID, чтобы получить доступ."
   - Кнопка "Войти через VK ID" → редиректит на VK OAuth:
     `https://id.vk.com/oauth2/auth?client_id=54657016&redirect_uri=https://vnutrenniy-kompas.ru/auth/callback&response_type=code&scope=vkid.person_email,phone`
   - После текста — ссылка "На главную" (`/`)
4. 'use client', тёмная тема через CSS переменные (--color-dark, --color-gold, --color-card, --color-text)
5. Стили в `<style>` JSX
6. Всё на русском

agents.result:
### SUMMARY
Создан файл `auth-gate.tsx` — клиентский React-компонент `AuthGate`, защищающий страницы от неавторизованных пользователей. При отсутствии токена (через `getAuthToken`) рендерится fullscreen-заглушка с заголовком, текстом, кнопкой «Войти через VK ID» (редирект на VK OAuth) и ссылкой «На главную». Тёмная тема реализована через CSS-переменные `--color-dark`, `--color-gold`, `--color-card`, `--color-text` с fallback-значениями. Компонент проходит `tsc --noEmit` без ошибок.

### EVIDENCE
- `src/lib/api.ts:18-21` — подтверждает сигнатуру `getAuthToken(): string | null` и её импорт из `'./api'`
- `npx tsc --noEmit` — exit code 0, ноль ошибок после правки типа `useState`

### CHANGES
- `src/lib/auth-gate.tsx` — создан новый файл (125 строк): компонент `AuthGate` с защитной логикой, стилями в `<style>` JSX и VK OAuth URL

### RISKS
- None observed.

### BLOCKERS
- None.
